title: aquilegia vulgaris subsp. paui
intro: Aquilegia vulgaris subsp. paui, also known as the Paui's columbine, is a subspecies of the columbine plant, which is a flowering perennial native to the Iberian Peninsula. The Paui's columbine has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Its health benefits include treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate pain. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is necessary to confirm its effectiveness and optimal preparation methods.
